---
title: "about_3"
author: "Edward"
date: "2025-03-04"
output: html_document
---

The **_GAM-MAP_** uses **GAMs**, which extend linear regression and generalised linear models by allowing flexible, nonlinear relationships among variables. This makes **GAMs** useful for exploring how climate factors influence malaria cases.


<div style="color:green; ">
1. What is GAM?
</div>
A predictive modeling approach that balances interpretability with flexibility, capturing both linear and nonlinear effects.

<div style="color:green; margin-top:10px;">
2. Building the GAM
</div>
In the app, <b>GAMs</b> are constructed by selecting relevant predictors (such as climate variables) and their interactions, and by applying smoothing functions to capture long-term trends (time), seasonal cycles (months), and regional patterns.

<div style="color:green; margin-top:10px;">
3. GAM Equations and Conditional Distributions
</div>
The app provides a simplified view of the <b>GAM</b> formulation, showing how predictors combine with smooth terms to model the relationship between climate variables and uncomplicated malaria cases. For full mathematical details, see the accompanying manuscript.
